prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG world atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Pathfinder / Golarion inspired epic political fantasy world map, dual-continent terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye atlas view with two major continents separated by uneven seas and narrow straits.
Subject: two asymmetric continents with an angled S-shaped inner sea channel between them, not a simple vertical split. Use diagonal and curved separation with island-studded straits, inland seas, peninsulas, gulf systems, capes, fjords, broken coasts, river deltas, and realistic curved shorelines. Include diverse biomes: desert regions, northern frozen wastes, jungle interiors, deep forests, wetlands, high mountain chains, harbor cities, ancient ruins, and trade routes.
Style/medium: illustrated Pathfinder-style fantasy world map, aged ink-on-parchment, political fantasy atlas with subtle border lines and small non-text heraldic markers, highly detailed hand-painted RPG sourcebook cartography.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape map, both continents fully visible, diagonal/S-shaped separation, generous ocean border, continent-scale geography.
Lighting/mood: epic political fantasy, rich nation-state borders, adventurer trade routes, old empire ruins, scheming factions and magical city-states.
Color palette: aged parchment, blue-green seas, readable biome zones for deserts, tundra, jungle, forests, wetlands, mountains, and coasts.
Materials/textures: parchment grain, engraved ink coastlines, hand-painted mountains and forests, fine atlas detail.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Avoid mirrored symmetry and avoid a clean vertical division.
